quiz.title: Unit 11 Quiz 1, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Introduction to Acids and Bases
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Acids and Bases Identification, Properties of Acids and Bases, Litmus Test, Reactions with Metals, Taste

questions.question: Which of the following is a base?
questions.explanation: NaOH (sodium hydroxide) is a base. HCl, H₂SO₄, and CH₃COOH are acids.

questions.question: Which of the following is a base?
questions.explanation: KOH (potassium hydroxide) is a base. HNO₃, H₃PO₄, and H₂CO₃ are acids.

questions.question: Which of the following is a base?
questions.explanation: LiOH (lithium hydroxide) is a base. HF and HNO₂ are acids, and SO₂ is an acidic oxide.

questions.question: Which of the following is a base?
questions.explanation: Ca(OH)₂ (calcium hydroxide) is a base. H₂O is neutral, CH₄ is neutral, and CO₂ is an acidic oxide.

questions.question: Acids are substances that produce ____________ in aqueous solution
questions.explanation: According to Arrhenius theory, acids produce hydrogen ions (H⁺) when dissolved in water.

questions.question: Acids react with water to produce __________
questions.explanation: When an acid dissolves in water, it donates a proton (H⁺) to water, forming hydronium ions (H₃O⁺).

questions.question: Acids react with metals to produce __________
questions.explanation: Many acids react with active metals (like Zn, Mg) to produce hydrogen gas and a salt.

questions.question: Acids turn
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus paper red. This is a common test for acidic solutions.

questions.question: Bases turn
questions.explanation: Bases turn red litmus paper blue. This is a common test for basic solutions.

questions.question: Acids have a _______ taste
questions.explanation: Acids typically have a sour taste, like citric acid in lemons or acetic acid in vinegar.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the basics of acids and bases! 🧪🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the properties and examples of acids and bases.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on identifying common acids and bases.
scoreMessages.0: Please review acid-base definitions, properties, and common examples.
